description: CLI interface for the AppyPie affiliate program

info.help: Show verified metadata for this CLI.
info.usage_instructions: Show the verified Appy Pie program metadata in JSON by default or as a key-value table with `--table`/`-t`.

program.usage_instructions: Use this group to inspect the read-only Appy Pie program metadata exposed by the CLI. `program info` is the canonical summary command.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

login.usage_instructions: Create or refresh the saved browser-session auth for a profile. Use `--profile`/`-p` to target a named profile, `--force`/`-F` to clear existing auth first, and `--credential-type`/`-c` when scoping the login flow.

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

logout.usage_instructions: Clear saved authentication for the selected profile. Use `--profile`/`-p` to target a non-default profile.

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test`` (API/OAuth) or via the live browser check (browser_session).

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

status.usage_instructions: Show authentication status across profiles in JSON or table form. Use `--profile`/`-p` for one profile and `--table`/`-t` for compact output.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Run the CLI authentication checks without normal business actions. Use `--profile`/`-p` for one profile, `--verbose`/`-v` for detailed checks, and `--table`/`-t` for table output.

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: List the saved auth profiles. Use `--table`/`-t` for compact output, `--limit`/`-l` to cap results, `--filter`/`-f` for field filtering, and `--properties`/`-p` to restrict returned fields.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

get.usage_instructions: Show metadata for one named auth profile. Pass the profile name as the required argument and add `--table`/`-t` for table output.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Create a new named auth profile from the CLI `.env.example` template. Pass the new profile name as the required argument.

set-default.help: Set a profile as the default (IS_DEFAULT_PROFILE=1).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to set as default

set-default.usage_instructions: Mark one named auth profile as the default profile the CLI should use. Pass the profile name as the required argument.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a named auth profile and its saved data. Pass the profile name as the required argument and use `--force`/`-F` to skip confirmation.

profiles.usage_instructions: Use these nested commands to create, inspect, list, set default, or delete named Appy Pie auth profiles.

auth.usage_instructions: Use these commands to manage the saved browser-session authentication profiles for Appy Pie.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Clear cached responses for the active profile. Add `--table`/`-t` for a compact summary when supported.

cache.usage_instructions: Use these commands to inspect or clear the local response cache for the CLI.

usage_instructions: Use this CLI to inspect Appy Pie program metadata and manage the local browser-session auth profile used by the tool. Start with `program info` for the read-only summary or `auth ...` to inspect/login/logout profiles.
